Great game ,Best open water sim there is , Biggest open world map in gaming .Sky is correct for any date , moon phases and constellations,Volumetric clouds and game engine deliver weather patterns , beautiful sunsets .Good looking game even 10 years later .
It can be( was for me ) a pain to get started . You need Wolves of steel Mod to get the most from it but its entirely playable unmodded.( i would recommend playing it unmodded first . 
   Modding the game is so easy and streamlined now as to what it used to be , So anyone who is going in now gets the advantage of years spent in the pursuit of a more perfect game . 
So why all the bad press ? 
The developers implicitly said in interviews that the game was to be a canvas , to be filled in by the modding community , 
  It was left , "unfinished" by design (This is a simplification ,Obviously there were many things at play as in also it was arguably released a bit early hardware wise , This game takes a good machine even 11 years later to run flawlessly .
  Which leads to a release to a public who aren't equipped to enjoy the game for one reason or the other and they basically killed the franchise with bad reviews . 
And , I have to admit . They had a point . Even today if you buy this on steam you will have to dl uconnect or whatever to dl the game to get it working , and its a lot of hoops you shouldnt have to deal with .
But .. when this game is working correctly its immersive and its laid back . You begin to think of the sub as a thing besides just a cursor . .Its on my list of essential PC games . But .. its like that weird singer you like , Not everyone is going to "Get It "...
I recommend it but its not for everyone .

Please do not waste money on this game! You can download and install and sometimes it works, but most of the time - when you start the game, you get: "Checking for updates" and then: "A service is not available" - finish - the game will not start.

I use a laptop, and use it from many different locations - meaning different dynamic IP addresses, but a game should not refuse to start because of that!

So - again, be careful - good game when it works, but most of the time it does not!

I love sub games and I've played all the Silent Hunters. I think 3 made the most sense. 4 was ruined by the constant air attacks. There always seems to be something unfinished about Ubisoft sub games. Silent Hunter 5 is no exception and makes you wonder why they can't seem to get it right after all the practice they've had making sub games.     
Pros: It's obvious that a great deal of creative energy and artistic talent went into making this game look stunning. Great graphics! Sea and weather effects are outstanding. Ships rendered extraordinarily well. Explosions, too. Well researched data, technologies, and history. My only tiny complaint with the graphics is with that too constant spray effect at the front of the boat.
Cons: I wish the same thought would have gone into the gameplay, which is terribly frustrating, sometimes stupid. You can't issue orders from the bridge. If you want to go to silent running you need to search for your Lieutenant down below and ask him to give the order. You can't even steer the boat from the bridge. The rudder control just gives you the option of five degrees to port" (or starboard) with the rudder amidships button unworkable, so if you are about to collide with a ship blocking the harbor entrance you have to jump to the map and reset your waypoints. When you tell your number 2 on the bridge to "Man the Flak guns" they get manned instantly but then those guys just stand there when the planes come and won't shoot. You need to man one of the guns yourself or else locate the plane with your binoculars and then hit spacebar when a little red x shows up...almost impossible to do with a fast moving plane. Mission orders keep repeating the same blather during each campaign phase. Mission objectives are confusing and often seem ludicrous...sometimes you have to look for that one Liberty Cargo ship in a 20 ship convoy to complete a mission. Really? With so many other more valuable targets in front of you? And when you DO find and sink that one ship that allows you to complete a mission objective there is a good chance that a glitch will keep it from being counted toward mission completion so you have to start that mission over again...it might be counted the next time, or not. And make sure you don't stop and attack a convoy until you dally in a certain area or all the ships you sink won't be counted either and all your torpedoes will be wasted. I never was able to complete all the mission chapters because of this glitch that didn't count my sinkings, no matter how many times i saved and started over. GOD, why can't Ubisoft at least let you progress to the other areas? Another frustration is that you are told to search a certain area and then are shown that the convoy you are supposed to be looking for is hundreds of miles away, heading away from you. The enemy AI is completely incompetent. Enemy planes show up often but they never attack. You will rarely be attacked by the protective destroyer escorts unless you are caught on the surface and then their gunnery is extremely accurate. They rarely use their sonar. You can get into the middle of any convoy with ease and do all the killing you want before they finally show up. Then all you have to do is go down and come up behind them and continue your killing spree. I have yet to be damaged by the even more rare depth charge. While the graphics are stupendous a little hand wringing with destroyers relentlessly hunting me down would have been a nice addition to the game (and a main reason for playing a sub game in the first place), something my first sub game had plenty of...Aces of the Deep. In reality sinking ships and sending men to their deaths in a frigid ocean is the height of evil cruelty but doing it in a game is addictive. Even so you'll soon tire of seeing another Liberty Cargo ship blow up and sink after doing all the steps necessary because it is so easy and there is little or no offensive action to stop you. BORING.   
You will hear that there is a mod out there called Wolves of Steel that fixes the game and makes it more realistic, but after jumping through 2001 hoops i finally got it installed and then nothing happened. Apparently the mod has a hard time dealing with the Steam version even tho the download process asks you to choose the Steam version. Yeah i did all the things subsim.com and Steam forums tells you to do to get the damn thing running, but nothing worked for me. So that wasted about a week of my life and made me angry with the mod people too for making it so complicated.Why can't somebody make a mod that you can just download and it works? 
So all that graphic work has been wasted by totally incompetent gameplay, which really makes me mad because this game could have been a really primo product...and still could be if somebody at Ubisoft cared and fixed it. What a waste!
Don't subject yourself to this beautiful mess.

TL:DR from a 15 year Silent Hunter 3 veteran: 
-Skipped vanilla and went straight for The Wolves Of Steel mod.
Game might be up your alley if you like to fiddle around and get things exactly how you want them the hard way but it's a fight and like me, you might not have the patience to spend time fixing Ubisofts mistakes.
I'm going back to Silent Hunter 3.

-Game has improvements over SH3 like graphics and crew interactions but many things have been made worse like the UI and all the keyboard shortcuts however to set controls to SH3 ones you need a mod which if you like WASD movement still isn't good enough since it uses the arrow keys.
-Most issues can be fixed with mods but many mods are very complex like NewUIs with TDC for SH5 by TheDarkWraith version 7.4.2 , I'm well able to fiddle around with this stuff and get it to a point where I'd be happy but I don't have the patience to mess around with that when I just want an idiot proof slap it together and boom it works solution.
-Crew animations are somewhat janky like for example, always climbing ladders at a 90 degree angle from what looks normal.(BIG sin considering Ubisoft had amazing animations in 2 Assassins Creed games prior to SH5's release.)

Suffice it to say there's enough issues that I'm going back to the tried and true Silent Hunter 3.  I've never played Silent Hunter 4 but Americans measuring depth in feet instead of proper meters like everyone else is a no from me here in Ireland.
The Game franchise is Great.

I was looking forward to a nicer experience than SH3. Installed this via Steam. Then the game would not start. Some uplay popped up. Ok, download uplay from Ubisoft. Then I had to register with Ubisoft, oh well. Started the game. Cannot pick my Nic as "Robert Schwarzenberg" is too long. Ok, shorten it. Start. I am supposed to go up the tower. So I try and try and no, wont move up. Checking support and "one has to look right into the ladder and move forward". Very intuitive, sure, a similar move like smashing your own teeth on steel. Doing the Intro and all good.
Then the mission picked, british coastal waters, sank about 60000 tons after getting more and more frustrated with the crappy Torpedos, one needs quite a few to sink these Uber-Merchant vessels. Alternativly use one, come up and then use the gun, how intuitive. And yes, this is a good move close to the british coast. 
But look and behold, my mission bar does not progress. Considering that the game only offers Type VII Boats, this was the final straw.
Never before have I seen such an unintuitive UI and such an abyssmal rework of a good old game, considering this is supposed to be a follow up to Silent Hunter 3.

Still one of the better submarine sims around. It's a slow pace game. But that is what life on a sub was like. And I personally like the slower pace. I'ts also an improvement that you can walk around on the sub and such. It makes everything feel more real. The game is a bit out of date, I know. I hope we can expect Silent Hunter 6 in the near future. I know there is a demand for this. In any case, this sim drags you down in the experience most of the time.

The game itself was promising, but budget cuts and a rushed development delivered an unfinished product, ridled with bugs. Thankfully, the Silent Hunter franchise has a long standing community, that transformed the game to something closer to what it should've been. I've played it very little without mods, and would advise against it, the vanilla game is playable but not to the same extent of enjoyability provided by mods. I personally use TWOS, sprinkled with a few other mods, and get a solid experience with an occasional bug here and there. This game is old, and it shows, but honestly i still think the graphics hold up. Don't buy expecting some super fancy simulator, nor a super laid back arcady "kill em all" style game.
If you are aware of the problems and familliar with modding, i'd recommend picking it up especially when on sale, as long as you're willing to go up the learning curve, especially if you're totally new to the genre. Regarding the ubisoft problems, i've been playing with no issues, but i use a modded version, so that might be the reason, since steam doesn't even log my gameplay hours (wayy more than 3).

More bugs than a roach motel, and more crashes than a demolition derby.  Maybe it's too old for the new computer I'm using, creating compatibility issues.  But between this, substandard training missions, and an overly tedious & complex way of talking to the crew, it's a disappointment.

I was really tough in my last comments. That's was based on being unable to download it! I found how and it installed perfectly. Playing the sim was the usual Ubisoft: full of promise......and bugs! I played the base software for about 8 hours and decided to install the megamod "The Wolves of Steel".  Playing in the TWoS universe drawn me in. Visuals in the external and internal are beautiful! There's a crew that is interactive to an extent. and 'quasi-lifelike'. They're better looking than the crash dummies that you see in other games. There's an extensive collection of navigation tools as an excellent TDC and many new features. If you're into sub/naval sims, Silent Hunter V is something you'll enjoy!  Caveat: you'll need a megamod to really get value and enjoyment.

I suppose mods could fix many of the issues with bugs, I still say look elsewhere, there are better subsims out there.

Don't let all the negative reviews pull you down on this game. It's far from a bad game especially with mods, which there are plenty of them on SUBSIM. Sure, it doesn't quite live up to the standard that Silent Hunter 3 set up, especially with the GWX supermod. 

I truly recommend that if you're interested in Silent Hunter 5, go ahead and get it, then install the Wolves of Steel mod, becomes just as fun as SH3 and fixes a lot of issue that the vanilla game had. You can combine a select few mods with your Wolves of Steel to create a truly amazing experience.

With the right mods, you get a subsim with amazing gameplay and for a 2010 game, it has amazing graphics. with 3d clouds and some of the best water effects I've seen in any game to date.

Overall, if you're getting into subsims, pick up SH5 with Wolves of Steel and SH3 with GWX, both amazing contenders in the subsim genre for just 20 bucks between the two, even cheaper if you bag them on sale.

After everything is done and installed, the only issues I have with SH5 is that Wolves of steel makes it strangely laggy, vanilla game had perfect performance with 60fps, but after WoS, performance decreased substantially.... still sorting it out as many people have their own workarounds to this since SH5 is still a 32bit application, and the other issue? NO TYPE IX! You command variants of the Type VII in SH5, from the VIIA to the VIIC/41. Unlike SH3, you won't be able to eventually play with a Type IX or XXI, which makes me a very sad Kaleun.

Having played this game for over 60 hrs total and having recently played again after a 5 year hiatus, I would call this game now unplayable due to the merchant tonnage bug, where merchant ships sunk, do not add to you mission total when sunk in the patrol area. Multiple trips back and forth from your base to the patrol area for no reward is infuriating. You should not have to mod a game to make it playable.
the game is rubbish, can't complete missions because the game does not save tonnage, stay away from this dung pile.
Could never get it to load, just got a black box then nothing. I was looking forward to playing this game as sub sims are so few in number. Would not recommend until this is fixed.

I have not played long, but this game is unbearable. 
When I purchased it, I was aware of issues and prepared to fix the bug with uplay that many users prevented to even play the game. So I made the fix suggested and started playing. I played SH3 and people are claiming SH5 is more casual friendly. It is on the contrahery. I am a casual player and I am struggling even on low difficulty, because of endless bugs. On my first mission I was searching for a convoy and speed up the game. Suddenly I get damage messages and the post credits come that I was sunk. It was that moment I saw why. On a clear day, being surfaced with full moral I rammed right into an escort. Penetrated the escort right through the middle. Only the moment I saw that, the crew reported the sighting of it. Perception check failed totally. Fun fact the guns of the escort (British) were aiming away from me. How the hell was that even possible? I never had bugs like this in SH3. Perfectly aimed torpedos doing strange spreads, even though I did not put in the commands for that. The graphics are good, but the gameplay is more than horribly. There are game-breaking and I can only recommend to not spend any money on this game from Buggysoft. Since I am above the hours eligible for refund, I will try to find mods to fix the massive lacking options to properly control the game and may try to play it after that.

Overall a great look at WWII submarine warfare from the German perspective. You can set the playstyle however for rookie players like myself it is a lot to wrap around. Quite the realistic game, so much so that my heart races every time i jump in.  I would recommend investing in a good computer to enjoy the best it has to offer.

I get sucked into playing this game every few years, once I start getting nostalgic for playing Silent Service on the Amiga. Sadly, the bugs that were there at launch are still there now and the more I play, the more I come across.

The submarine, the star of the show, comes so close to feeling alive and then falls short. The crew just keep repeating dialogue and switching to "battle stations" makes no discernible difference. It just feels like the entire game was a project that got to 90% finished and then they got bored and published it.

Make sure you have the wolves of steel installed. It is fun to play real navigation for a challenge but easy to play manual aiming off (from the options screen). Then you lock with space bar, unlock to set torpedo gyro automatically, and fire the torpedo.
Buggy to the point where it just takes away any measure of fun from you!!

So I have played this game for almost 100 hours and have never once been able to finish it. It almost is always ruined by some bug or glitch or something. Most of the time I'll end up going back and re-playing a section because of some crash. To add on to that when it crashes you can't simply close it, it often forces you to either log out or completely restart. 
It has potential but honestly the bugs and glitches make it a terrible game. I've tried it with and without mods, still the same result. 
I DO NOT RECOMMEND BUYING THIS GAME.
